title: DownloadRunsSchema
description: Per-download-run bookkeeping for app-managed sources. Each invocation of a downloader (`python -m download.<provider>_web …`) writes one row here before it starts pulling and updates it on completion. The downloader's payload lands in `<root>/raw/<source>/<ISO-timestamp>.<ext>`; ingest treats `WHERE ingested_at IS NULL` as its work queue. See plan-ui-driven-sync.md, Phases B + C.

DownloadRunRow.description: One row per downloader invocation. The downloader inserts at start; the same downloader stamps `finished_at` on success; the ingest pipeline stamps `ingested_at` + `doc_uuids_touched` after it consumes the raw file. Rows are kept indefinitely so we can answer `which sync brought in this document?`

id.description: UUIDv4 generated by the downloader at run start. Used as the FK on any future per-document provenance tracking.

raw_path.description: Path to the raw file produced by this run, relative to `<root>/raw/`. The file is named `<ISO-timestamp>.<ext>` so disk listing is chronologically sorted.

kind.description: `full` = complete snapshot of everything the downloader could see (used as a periodic self-healing checkpoint); `delta` = items new/changed since the previous run, using the last successful run's `finished_at` as the `since` watermark. Default mode is `delta`; `--full` overrides.

started_at.description: When the downloader began this run (ISO-8601 with explicit local offset, per AGENTS.md).

finished_at.description: When the downloader finished successfully (ISO-8601 with explicit offset). NULL means the run is either still in progress or it crashed; ingest treats NULL as `do not consume yet`.

ingested_at.description: When ingest finished consuming `raw_path` (ISO-8601 with explicit offset). NULL until then. Ingest's work queue is `SELECT ... WHERE finished_at IS NOT NULL AND ingested_at IS NULL`.

markdown_uuids_touched.description: JSON array of `markdowns.markdown_uuid` values that this run produced changes for. Populated by ingest when it sets `ingested_at`. Useful for `which sync surfaced this thread?` provenance queries; not used by the hot path.
